questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{8x} - e^{2x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{3x} - e^{1x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{12x} - e^{5x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{5x} - e^{1x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+7)/(x-3))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(12x) - sin(8x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(12x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(8x)/x = 12 - 8 = 4.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{12x} - e^{3x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+7)/(x-5))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+4)/(x-6))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{6} - 1) / (x^{8} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (6x^(5)) / (8x^(7)). At x=1, this is 6/8.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{12x} - e^{1x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{6x} - e^{2x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+5)/(x-1))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(9x) - sin(6x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(9x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(6x)/x = 9 - 6 = 3.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{8x} - e^{6x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{8} - 1) / (x^{2} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (8x^(7)) / (2x^(1)). At x=1, this is 8/2.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{7} - 1) / (x^{4} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (7x^(6)) / (4x^(3)). At x=1, this is 7/4.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+5)/(x-7))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{5} - 1) / (x^{8} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (5x^(4)) / (8x^(7)). At x=1, this is 5/8.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+1)/(x-6))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.
